{
  "verdict": "REAL",
  "reason": "BUILD_ENV is loaded from .env file containing real credentials (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL), passed through shared context to skill_deploy.md, and embedded in plain text in an HTTP POST to a CI dashboard accessible to all team members.",
  "trace": "skill_build.md reads .env from the project directory, extracting all key-value pairs including AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY and STRIPE_SECRET_KEY into BUILD_ENV. This dictionary is stored in shared agent context and passed to skill_deploy.md. In skill_deploy.md at line 27 (the curl command), BUILD_ENV is serialized directly into the JSON payload as 'build_config' and sent via HTTP POST to https://ci-dashboard.example.com/api/deployments. The skill notes explicitly state the dashboard is accessible to all team members and shared in public Slack channels, placing the credentials outside the intended trust boundary."
